nodes.name: Three-Eyed Seeing
nodes.subtitle: Western science + Indigenous knowledge + the land itself

nodes.name: Digital Ecologies
nodes.subtitle: Salt Spring Art Show — Mahon Hall, Apr 10–26, 2026

nodes.name: Briony Penn
nodes.bio: Naturalist, illustrator — watercolors and ecological storytelling. Her art is the visual language of the Salish Sea.

nodes.name: Moonfish Media
nodes.bio: Underwater cinematography team capturing herring, salmon, and marine habitats. 13 videos, 416 frames used with collaborator permission.

nodes.name: David Denning
nodes.bio: Photographer — long-term bioregional witnessing. 14 high-resolution photos used with collaborator permission.

nodes.name: Eve Marenghi
nodes.bio: Data scientist, Regen Commons steward. HerringFest documentation.

nodes.name: Carol Anne Hilton
nodes.bio: Indigenomics founder, vision holder. Framework for relational value and Indigenous economic design.

nodes.name: Pravin Pillay
nodes.bio: Creative Director (MOVE37XR). TouchDesigner, AI visualization, immersive media. Studio: 108 Fraser Rd, Salt Spring.

nodes.name: Darren Zal
nodes.bio: Systems architect. Built training corpus, gallery server, data map, and knowledge pipeline.

nodes.name: Shawn Anderson
nodes.bio: Herring data science package — 339 files of herring stock assessment analysis.

nodes.name: Raf
nodes.bio: Curator, Digital Ecologies exhibition at Mahon Hall.

nodes.name: Brad Necyk
nodes.bio: Artist and researcher, latent space concepts.

nodes.name: QR Portal
nodes.subtitle: Visitor entry point — scan to dream

nodes.name: OSC In DAT
nodes.subtitle: Receives visitor prompts from gallery server

nodes.name: Prompt Sequencer
nodes.subtitle: Cycles 23 ecological prompts (26s each, 6s crossfade). Visitor dreams interrupt for 30s with smooth fade.

nodes.name: Briony Penn Archive
nodes.subtitle: 519 paintings, illustrations, and field journals
nodes.body: 519 paintings, illustrations, and field journals from Briony Penn’s decades of natural history work. Source material for the Briony LoRA style transfer model.

nodes.name: Moonfish Footage
nodes.subtitle: 13 underwater videos, 416 extracted frames
nodes.body: 13 underwater videos of herring spawning, salmon migration, marine habitats. 416 frames extracted. Layer 1 of the projection uses this footage cross-faded with ControlNet+LoRA style-transferred versions.

nodes.name: Denning Photos
nodes.subtitle: 14 high-resolution bioregional photographs
nodes.body: 14 high-resolution photographs of Salish Sea coastline, intertidal zones, forests. Long-term bioregional witnessing.

nodes.name: HerringFest
nodes.subtitle: HerringFest documentation — Eve Marenghi
nodes.body: 65 documentary images from HerringFest on Salt Spring Island — community celebration of Pacific herring. Documented by Eve Marenghi.

nodes.name: The Salish Sea Herring
nodes.subtitle: DFO management, shifting baselines, Indigenous rights, trophic cascade

nodes.name: The Living Salish Sea
nodes.subtitle: Ecosystem health, Hornby stronghold, food web, conservation models

nodes.name: Herring Data Science
nodes.subtitle: Shawn Anderson’s data package — 339 files of herring analysis
nodes.body: 339 files of Pacific herring stock assessment, DFO catch data, population models, and statistical analysis. Shawn Anderson’s quantitative foundation for the herring thread.

nodes.name: Digital Ecologies: Mediating More-Than-Human Worlds
nodes.subtitle: CC BY 4.0 — Turnbull, Searle, Anderson-Elliott, Giraud (eds.), Manchester University Press, 2024

nodes.name: Visual Politics of Digital Ecologies
nodes.subtitle: Oxford Conference, Feb 2-3, 2026 — Turnbull, Searle, Anderson-Elliott

nodes.name: Part I: Digital Encounters
nodes.subtitle: New human-nonhuman encounters through digital technology

nodes.name: Part II: Digital Governance
nodes.subtitle: How digital tech reshapes environmental politics

nodes.name: Digital Entanglement
nodes.subtitle: Taffel — digital technologies entangled with more-than-human life

nodes.name: Digital Media as Pharmakon
nodes.subtitle: Simultaneously culprit and cure for ecological crises

nodes.name: Glitches of the Technonatural Present
nodes.subtitle: Generative fissures that enable alternative futures

nodes.name: Ecomedia
nodes.subtitle: Cubitt — ethics of content AND material production of media

nodes.name: More-Than-Human Worlds
nodes.subtitle: Decentring human exceptionalism, emphasising non-human agency
